heading: Business workforce partnerships for job skill training in high-growth occupations or industries

children.text: provide relevant job skill training in high-growth and high-wage industries or occupations to nontraditional students; and
strengthen ties between degree credit offerings at institutions of higher education and business and industry workforce needs.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: From the amounts appropriated under subsection (k), the Secretary shall award grants, on a competitive basis, to eligible partnerships for the purpose provided in subsection (a).

children.heading: Duration
children.label: 2 Duration
children.text: The Secretary shall award grants under this section for a period of not less than 36 months and not more than 60 months.

children.heading: Supplement, not supplant
children.label: 3 Supplement, not supplant
children.text: Funds made available under this section shall be used to supplement, and not supplant, other Federal, State, and local funds available to the eligible partnership for carrying out the activities described in subsection (c).

expand or create for-credit academic programs or programs of training that provide relevant job skill training for high-growth and high-wage occupations or industries, including offerings connected to registered apprenticeship programs and entrepreneurial training opportunities;

in consultation with faculty in the appropriate departments of an institution of higher education, adapt college offerings to the schedules and needs of working students, such as the creation of evening, weekend, modular, compressed, or distance learning formats;
purchase equipment that will facilitate the development of academic programs or programs of training that provide training for high-growth and high-wage occupations or industries;

strengthen outreach efforts that enable students, including students with limited English proficiency, to attend institutions of higher education with academic programs or programs of training focused on high-growth and high-wage occupations or industries;
expand worksite learning and training opportunities, including registered apprenticeships as appropriate; and
support other activities the Secretary determines to be consistent with the purpose of this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each eligible partnership that desires a grant under this section shall submit an application to the Secretary at such time, in such manner, and accompanied by such additional information as the Secretary may require.

children.label: A
children.text: how the eligible partnership, through the institution of higher education, will provide relevant job skill training for students to enter high-growth and high-wage occupations or industries; and

children.label: B
children.text: how the eligible partnership has consulted with employers and, where applicable, labor organizations to identify local high-growth and high-wage occupations or industries.
give priority to applications focused on serving nontraditional students;
ensure an equitable distribution of grant funds under this section among urban and rural areas of the United States; and

children.label: A
children.text: offer one- or two-year high-quality programs of instruction and job skill training for students entering a high-growth and high-wage occupation or industry;

children.label: B
children.text: involve the local business community, and to place graduates in employment in high-growth and high-wage occupations or industries in the community; and

children.label: C
children.text: serve adult workers or displaced workers.

children.heading: Administrative costs
children.label: f Administrative costs
children.text: A grantee under this section may use not more than five percent of the grant amount to pay administrative costs associated with activities funded by the grant.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: g Technical assistance
children.text: The Secretary shall provide technical assistance to grantees under this section throughout the grant period.

children.heading: Evaluation
children.label: h Evaluation
children.text: The Secretary shall conduct an evaluation of the effectiveness of the program under this section based on performance standards developed in consultation with the Department of Labor, and shall disseminate to the public the findings of such evaluation and information related to promising practices developed under this section.

for changes to this chapter and related Acts, such as the Carl D. Perkins Career and Technical Education Act of 2006 [ et seq.] and the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (including titles I and II [ et seq., 3271 et seq.]), to help create and sustain business and industry workforce partnerships at institutions of higher education; and20 U.S.C. 230129 U.S.C. 3111

for other changes to this chapter and related Acts to otherwise strengthen the links between business and industry workforce needs, workforce development programs, and other degree credit offerings at institutions of higher education.

children.label: i
children.text: one or more institutions of higher education, one of which serves as the fiscal agent and grant recipient for the eligible partnership;

children.label: ii
children.text: except as provided in subparagraph (B), an employer, group of employers, local board (as such term is defined in section 3 of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act []), or workforce intermediary, or any combination thereof; and29 U.S.C. 3102

children.label: iii
children.text: where applicable, one or more labor organizations that represent workers locally in the businesses or industries that are the focus of the partnership, including as a result of such an organization’s representation of employees at a worksite at which the partnership proposes to conduct activities under this section.

children.heading: State and local boards
children.label: B State and local boards
children.text: Notwithstanding subparagraph (A), if an institution of higher education that is participating in an eligible partnership under this section is located in a State that does not operate local boards, an eligible partnership may include a State board (as such term is defined in section 3 of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act []).29 U.S.C. 3102

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: C Rule of construction
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed to prohibit an eligible partnership that is in existence on , from applying for a grant under this section.August 14, 2008

children.label: A
children.text: who is an independent student, as defined in ;section 1087vv(d) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: on less than a full-time basis;

children.label: ii
children.text: via evening, weekend, modular, or compressed courses; or

children.label: iii
children.text: via distance education methods; and

children.label: i
children.text: enrolled for the first time in an institution of higher education three or more years after completing high school; or

children.label: ii
children.text: works full-time.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: k Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated to carry out this section such sums as may be necessary for fiscal year 2009 and each of the five succeeding fiscal years.
